rulings.Case Title: ALIX SANTA-IGLESIA et al VS. BALWANTSINH THAKOR, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint To Add Additional Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, line 1. PLAINTIFFs ALIX SANTA-IGLESIA, DEBORAH BASS, DANIEL LEE, WILLIAM CABRAL, CLYDE ROSE, and JAMES HARRIS' Amended Motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint To Add Additional Plaintiffs. Grant subject to continuance of trial. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EMELIE B SANTANA VS. BANK OF AMERICA HOME LOANS et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, line 2. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustain with leave to amend to allege facts: re tolling regarding causes of action 2, 3, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12-15, 17, and 22; re cause of action 19 to allege duty and breach of it; re cause of action 8 to allege terms of contract breached; re causes of action 3, 6, 12, and 14 to allege fraud with particularity and differentiating between defendants; re cuases of action 11 and 16 to reallege as remedy and or doctrine based on other causes of action; re causes of action 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, 18, 19 and 20 to allege tender or exemption from it. Overrule causes of action 1 and 4 not addressed in points and authorities. Sustain without leave to amend cause of action 21 not addressed in opposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MORRISON VS. MERIDIAN FORECLOSURE SERVICE et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, line 4. DEFENDANT OWB REO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustain with leave to amend to allege actionable violations other than purported violations alleged in first amended complaint and allege tender and prejudice; Plaintiff fails to 1) provide any authority for proposition that assignment of Deed of Trust needs to be recorded to be effective; 2) Plaintiff's reading of CC 2924(g) is not plausible. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES MORRISON VS. MERIDIAN FORECLOSURE SERVICE et al

DEFENDANT ONEWEST BANK, FSB's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustain with leave to amend to allege actionable violations other than purported violations alleged in first amended complaint and allege tender and prejudice; Plaintiff fails to 1) provide any authority for proposition that assignment of Deed of Trust needs to be recorded to be effective; 2) Plaintiff's reading of CC 2924(g) is not plausible. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN FLOOD et al VS. JAMES YOUNG et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, line 5. DEFENDANTs JAMES YOUNG, and BETTY YOUNG's AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Deny. Moving party failed to show that service of notice of rent increase was "in connection with a public issue or an issue of public interest." Moving party concedes that there are no legal authorities on point. Reply 2:22-23. Notice involved interests of one landlord and one tenant in connection to one property. But for this litigation Notice would not be published to any third parties. Thus, service of Notice could not directly effect a large number of people beyond the direct participants. Assertion of broad and amorphous public interest to past controversy involving Costa-Hawkins Act is insufficient; moving party does not assert that there is a present wide-spread interest in Costa-Hawkins. Reply 5:16. Moving party also does not explain how its Notice contributed to this past controversy or was even connected to it. Finally, the focus of moving party's conduct was rent increase for one tenant, not the validity of rent increase statutes. See Cross v. Cooper (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 357; Weinberg v. Feisel (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1122; and Wilbanks v. Wolk (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 883. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. MANUELA ALVAREZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, line 6. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Grant, subject to opposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ZHAO XIONG LI VS. CHOI LAN LEUNG et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, line 7. DEFENDANTs CHOI LEUNG, KA LO's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustain without leave to amend. Original notice failed to comply with 37.9(c) and admits only oral advice given and has changed original notice by now adding 37.9(c) language improperly. = (501/REQ)
